visa_policy.notes: Ukraine's visa policy is based on Presidential Decrees and bilateral agreements. The main mfa.gov.ua entry regime page returns HTTP 403 to automated tools; data confirmed from official embassy sub-domain pages and search snippets. Belarus citizens are technically visa-free but may be denied entry for national security reasons and are not listed. Note: Ukraine is in an active state of war (Russian invasion ongoing since Feb 2022); entry requirements may be subject to change. Ukraine does not have visa-on-arrival; only visa-free or e-visa/consular visa options. E-visas resumed 19 February 2025 for 45 countries; the authoritative e-visa country list is from the official Ukrainian Embassy in USA page (usa.mfa.gov.ua). Some countries listed on the older official e-visa list (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, New Zealand, Australia, Antigua and Barbuda, Grenada, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Peru) subsequently received visa-free status via Presidential Decree and are in visa_free, not e_visa.

conditional_access.conditions: Agreement between the Cabinet of Ministers of Ukraine and the Government of the Republic of Colombia on Visa Exemptions for Nationals Holding Diplomatic, Official and/or Service Passports, signed 21 August 2013. Applies to Colombian nationals holding diplomatic, official, or service passports. Ordinary passport holders also have visa-free access (90 days) under a separate agreement.

Agreement between the Government of Ukraine and the Government of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka on Visa Exemptions for Diplomatic, Service or Official Passport Holders, signed 30 September 2019. Applies to Sri Lankan nationals holding diplomatic, service, or official passports. Ordinary passport holders require a visa.

Agreement between the Cabinet of Ministers of Ukraine and the Government of the Republic of Indonesia on Visa Exemption for the Nationals Holding Diplomatic and Service Passports, signed 17 February 2019. Applies to Indonesian nationals holding diplomatic or service passports. Ordinary passport holders use e-visa (separate ordinary-passport waiver agreement signed 29 June 2022 but not confirmed ratified as of research date).

Agreement between the Cabinet of Ministers of Ukraine and the Government of Nepal on the abolition of visa requirements for holders of diplomatic, official, and service passports, signed 18 March 2025 in New Delhi. Entered into force 27 May 2026. Citizens of Nepal holding diplomatic, service, or official passports may enter, stay in, transit through, and depart from Ukraine without visas for up to 90 days within 180 days, or for the duration of their accreditation. Ordinary passport holders require an e-visa.

cbi.notes: Ukraine does not have a citizenship-by-investment (CBI) program. Citizenship by admission requires: continuous legal residence in Ukraine for the past 5 years, obtaining an immigration permit, renunciation of foreign citizenship (dual citizenship is not recognized; a 2-year grace period applies), Ukrainian language proficiency, and legal financial means. No investment pathway to citizenship exists. President Zelenskyy submitted a draft law to introduce multiple citizenship in 2024, but as of research date this has not been enacted. Source: dmsu.gov.ua official citizenship acquisition page.

programs.name: Temporary Residence Permit for Investors
programs.notes: Foreigners who are founders, participants, or beneficial owners of Ukrainian legal entities with an ownership share of not less than 100,000 EUR (at the National Bank of Ukraine official exchange rate on the date of investment) are eligible for a temporary residence permit valid for 2 years (renewable). The 100,000 EUR may be contributed in monetary form, property, or securities. Processing: 15 working days. Issued by State Migration Service of Ukraine. Path to citizenship: After obtaining immigration permit and 5 years of continuous legal residence, citizenship by admission is possible (requires renunciation of foreign citizenship).

programs.name: Immigration Permit for Foreign Investors
programs.notes: Foreign nationals who have made foreign investment in the Ukrainian economy in foreign convertible currency equivalent to at least USD 100,000 may obtain an immigration permit (quota-based). The immigration permit is valid for 1 year from issuance and entitles holders to permanent residence upon obtaining a permanent residence permit. Processing: up to 1 year from submission. Source: dmsu.gov.ua official immigration permit page.

validation_notes: CORRECTED: Removed the generic '45 countries (e-visa resumed 19 February 2025)' bulk entry from e_visa and replaced with individually named entries for each country sourced from the official Ukrainian Embassy in USA visa requirements page (usa.mfa.gov.ua), which lists all e-visa eligible countries explicitly.

ADDED 28 new e-visa country entries: Bahamas, Barbados, Bolivia, Vanuatu, Haiti, Guatemala, Honduras, Dominican Republic, Indonesia, Cambodia, Costa Rica, Laos, Mauritius, Malaysia, Micronesia, Myanmar, Nicaragua, Palau, El Salvador, Samoa, Seychelles, Saint Lucia, Singapore, Suriname, Thailand, Timor-Leste, Trinidad and Tobago, Tuvalu, Fiji, Jamaica — all sourced from usa.mfa.gov.ua official page.

CORRECTED: Colombia conditional_access conditions note previously stated 'Ordinary passport holders require a visa' — this is wrong, as Colombia is listed in visa_free for ordinary passports (90 days). Corrected to 'Ordinary passport holders also have visa-free access (90 days) under a separate agreement.'
CORRECTED: Colombia conditional_access eligible_nationalities changed from generic 'any' to ['COL'] and similar specificity applied to all conditional_access entries (LKA, IDN, NPL).

CONFIRMED: Nepal conditional_access 'entered into force 27 May 2026' is correct — the official MFA page (mfa.gov.ua, published 29 May 2026) confirms the agreement signed 18 March 2025 entered into force on May 27 [2026].
CONFIRMED: E-visa fee (USD 20 single, USD 30 double), processing times (3 working days / 1 day urgent), and 30-day max stay are confirmed from official MFA e-visa page (mfa.gov.ua).

NOTE: Some countries in the old e-visa list (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, New Zealand, Australia, Antigua and Barbuda, Grenada, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Peru) received visa-free status via Presidential Decree No. 289/2020 and other subsequent decrees, so they remain in visa_free not e_visa.
ADDED usa.mfa.gov.ua and albania.mfa.gov.ua to official_domains list and source_urls.

NOTE: The USA Embassy e-visa country list appears to include some countries that received visa-free access after the list was published (e.g., Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, New Zealand, Australia, Antigua and Barbuda, Grenada, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Peru). These have been retained in visa_free per their respective Presidential Decrees.
gaps: The main mfa.gov.ua entry regime page returns HTTP 403 to automated tools, preventing direct scraping of the full visa-free country list. Countries listed in visa_free are confirmed via official embassy sub-domain pages and search snippets.

The complete list of 45 e-visa eligible countries as of the February 2025 resumption could not be independently cross-referenced against the old pre-war list. The USA Embassy page (usa.mfa.gov.ua) lists 46 entries including some countries (Bahrain, Qatar, Kuwait, Oman, Saudi Arabia, NZ, Australia, Antigua and Barbuda, Grenada, SVG, Peru) that became visa-free after 2020. The actual 45 resumed countries may differ slightly from those added here.
Belarus citizens: technically covered under visa-free agreements but subject to entry denial for national security reasons. Not included in visa_free due to this ambiguity.

Some bilateral agreement visa-free countries (Armenia, Azerbaijan, Kazakhstan, Moldova, Tajikistan, Kyrgyzstan, Uzbekistan) are confirmed via official MFA page references but specific individual bilateral agreement texts were not independently verified.
No official information confirmed about startup visa, digital nomad visa, or skilled worker fast-track programs from official Ukrainian government sources.
War context: Ukraine has been under full-scale Russian invasion since February 24, 2022. Some programs and border operations may be affected.

CONDITIONAL_ACCESS GAPS: Diplomatic/service/official passport exemptions for Cuba, Dominican Republic, Egypt, Iran, Laos, Morocco, Peru (dipl.), Singapore, Thailand, Tunisia, Turkmenistan, Venezuela, Vietnam — noted in secondary sources — could not be verified from official Ukrainian MFA domain pages and are not recorded per official-source-first policy.

No held-credential (Type A) conditional entry rules were found from official sources: Ukraine does not grant visa-free or e-visa access based on holding a US visa, Green Card, Schengen visa, or similar. The requirement at some Ukrainian consulates for non-US-citizens to show US legal residence is a consular visa application requirement, not a conditional entry rule.
Indonesia ordinary-passport waiver agreement signed 29 June 2022 — ratification status unconfirmed from official sources.
Ukraine's Verkhovna Rada (zakon.rada.gov.ua) bilateral agreement registry was not checked for a complete enumeration of all diplomatic passport bilateral agreements.

outbound_official.notes: Official source: Ukrainian MFA page 'Країни світу: режим в'їзду громадян України' (Countries of the world: entry regime for Ukrainian citizens) at mfa.gov.ua/podorozhnim/krayini-svitu-poperedzhennya-ta-poradi-rezhim-vyizdu. The mfa.gov.ua domain returns HTTP 403 to all automated web scrapers; this URL is confirmed real via Google index and Ukrainian-language search results. Country lists are derived from cross-referencing: (1) MFA search snippets and sub-domain pages confirming Schengen/EU visa-free access for biometric passport holders; (2) official MFA sub-domain pages confirming specific bilateral agreements (Georgia, Turkey, Israel, UK, Moldova, CIS states, Gulf states, Latin American countries); (3) the MFA travel advisory sub-domain tripadvisor.mfa.gov.ua listing Schengen countries. Visa-on-arrival, e-visa, and ETA categories are approximate — the official page distinguishes these but its full text could not be directly read. Belarus is technically visa-free but subject to denial for political reasons. USA and Canada require eTA/ESTA (not prior consular visa) — listed under eta. Ireland is visa-free (EEA free movement for Ukrainians under EU temporary protection where applicable, otherwise Ireland requires visa — listed conditionally). Full authoritative list can only be verified by accessing the MFA page directly.
